title: Switzerland vs Qatar Predictions, Picks & Odds — Saturday, June 13 World Cup Match
original_text:
Switzerland vs Qatar Predictions, Picks & Odds — Saturday, June 13 World Cup Match
13+ years betting experience
Updated: Jun 12, 2026 , 07:41 PM ET • 4 min read
Switzerland opens its World Cup campaign against a Qatar side facing a significant step up in class. We break down whether the Swiss can turn their advantage on paper into a statement victory.
Jump to section:
Photo By - Reuters Connect. Switzerland's Dan Ndoye celebrates scoring their third goal.
The past month has seen Switzerland overtake Canada as the favorites to win Group B, and they have a chance to claim three points and establish a strong goal differential in Saturday’s fixture against Qatar.
Qatar has played two matches in the past six months, and my Switzerland vs. Qatar predictions explain why the Swiss are likely to score.
Read on for my World Cup picks for Saturday, June 13, with kickoff set for 3:00 p.m. ET on FOX.
TOP PICK
Who will win Switzerland vs Qatar: Switzerland -2.5 (+161)
SUI @ QAT Match starts: 9 hrs
SPREAD
SUI -2.5 (+161)
Trade now
All 3 SUI vs QAT Picks
Qatar was forced to cancel spring friendlies with Argentina, Serbia, and Sudan due to the ongoing Middle East conflict.
As a result, they’ve played just twice since early December of 2025. That lack of time together was evident in their May 28 friendly with Ireland and their friendly against El Salvador this past weekend.
El Salvador is ranked 100th in the FIFA rankings, but they were by far the better side. Qatar managed just one effort on goal, and looked incohesive despite nearly 60% possession.
The World Cup odds have Switzerland heavily favored as the more talented team in a strong run of form. They’ve lost one of 14 matches since the start of 2025, with the last four outings coming against World Cup opponents.
They have proven high-level talent in all four aspects of their 4-2-3-1 setup, led in the midfield by Granit Xhaka’s poise and playmaking.
Qatar have struggled to defend centrally on a consistent basis, and Johan Manzambi will give them fits with his creativity.
Switzerland should dominate the ball and win it back quickly, which means plenty of possession and goal-scoring opportunities. With goal differential being a key tiebreaker, I’m expecting the Swiss to pour it on and win this one easily.
I would play it north of +150.

Switzerland vs Qatar Over/Under Pick: Over 2.5 (-140)
SUI @ QAT Match starts: 9 hrs
TOTAL
o2.5 (-140)
Trade now
All 3 SUI vs QAT Picks
I’m not expecting Qatar to score in this match, which is why I’m favoring the spread over the total—but obviously the total will hit if the spread does.
Everything that Qatar will look to do offensively will operate through Akram Afif. Whether he lines up centrally or on the left, his pace gives them a strong outlet on the counter-attack.
That said, Swiss RB Silvan Widmer loves to push up in attack. If Nico Elvedi and Manuel Akanji don’t properly cover that space when he vacates it, it allows Qatar a path to a goal.
Qatar has leaked scoring chances and goals since the 2022 World Cup. Their back line and midfield all play domestically in Qatar, and are simply overmatched in terms of talent.
That can be overcome by a solid structure, but Qatar lacks that. It’s a key reason why there’s a massive gap between their ELO ranking of 97th and their FIFA ranking of 55th.
The Swiss bagged three goals vs. Germany in the spring and defeated Sweden 4-1 in World Cup qualifying last fall. Their recent friendly with Jordan also saw them claim a 4-1 win.
They can carry this total on their own, and I’ll back the Over to -150.
Switzerland vs Qatar projected lineup
Switzerland (4-2-3-1): Kobel; Widmer, Elvedi, Akanji, Rodriguez; Freuler, Xhaka; Ndoye, Aebischer, Manzambi; Amdouni
Qatar (4-3-3): Abunada; Al Oui, Miguel, Khoukhi, Elamin; Gaber, Fathi, Laye; Abdurisag, Afif, Edmilson Junior
Switzerland vs Qatar injuries
Switzerland: No injuries to report.
Qatar: No injuries to report.
Switzerland vs Qatar weather
Weather conditions at Levi's Stadium are expected to be about 79 degrees and sunny with no rain.
